patient_text: A 68-year-old man with a long smoking history presents with painless gross hematuria. CT urography reveals a mass in the bladder wall.
criterion_text: Has already initiated curative treatment for the current episode of cancer

patient_text: A 60-year-old woman presents with progressive dyspnea, dry cough, and clubbing of the fingers. Chest CT reveals bilateral basal reticular opacities with honeycombing.
criterion_text: Meets either of the following: the patient has been on a stable dose of nintedanib or pirfenidone for at least 8 weeks prior to screening and during screening (nintedanib ≥100 mg BID, pirfenidone ≥400 mg TID, no dose changes), tolerates the treatment, and plans to continue this background therapy during the study; OR the patient has not received nintedanib or pirfenidone for at least 4 weeks prior to screening and during screening (previous treatment discontinued or treatment-naïve), and does not plan to initiate or re-initiate nintedanib or pirfenidone during the study.

patient_text: A 29-year-old woman presents with intermittent palpitations, heat intolerance, and unintentional weight loss. Physical exam reveals a fine tremor, tachycardia, and a diffusely enlarged, non-tender thyroid gland.
criterion_text: Thyroid hormones within normal range without anti thyroid drugs

patient_text: A 19-year-old male presents with fever, sore throat, fatigue, and posterior cervical lymphadenopathy. Physical exam reveals splenomegaly, and a monospot test is positive.
criterion_text: Serology compatible with primary EBV infection (anti-EBNA IgG negative, anti-VCA IgG negative, anti-VCA IgM positive OR anti-EBNA IgG negative, anti-VCA IgG positive, anti-VCA IgM positive)

patient_text: A 19-year-old male presents with fever, sore throat, fatigue, and posterior cervical lymphadenopathy. Physical exam reveals splenomegaly, and a monospot test is positive.
criterion_text: No ME/CFS (ICD-10 G93.3)

patient_text: A 54-year-old man with a history of chronic alcohol use presents with severe epigastric pain radiating to the back, nausea, and vomiting. Labs reveal markedly elevated serum lipase and amylase.
criterion_text: Functional hyposplenism or asplenia, patient under long-term antibiotic prophylaxis for this reason

patient_text: A 68-year-old man with a long smoking history presents with painless gross hematuria. CT urography reveals a mass in the bladder wall.
criterion_text: Resides in a rural county served by the specified hospital sites with Rural-Urban Continuum Code (RUCC) 4-9

patient_text: A 68-year-old man with a long smoking history presents with painless gross hematuria. CT urography reveals a mass in the bladder wall.
criterion_text: At least 1 measurable lesion according to RECIST v1.1, which (in the Investigator's opinion) has had objective radiological progression on or after the last therapy, or at least one assessable lesion e.g. pleural or peritoneal thickening that does not fulfil RECIST v1.1 criteria for measurable disease.

patient_text: A 54-year-old man with a history of chronic alcohol use presents with severe epigastric pain radiating to the back, nausea, and vomiting. Labs reveal markedly elevated serum lipase and amylase.
criterion_text: Aplasia defined by circulating neutrophil count < 500 cells/mm³

patient_text: A 29-year-old woman presents with intermittent palpitations, heat intolerance, and unintentional weight loss. Physical exam reveals a fine tremor, tachycardia, and a diffusely enlarged, non-tender thyroid gland.
criterion_text: If patient: Graves' disease with positive TSH-receptor antibodies and thyroid hormones above the upper reference limit at diagnosis

patient_text: A 3-month-old infant with projectile non-bilious vomiting after feeding, visible peristalsis, and a palpable olive-shaped mass in the epigastrium. Labs show hypochloremic, hypokalemic metabolic alkalosis.
criterion_text: Minor or adult patient ≥ 4 years of age

patient_text: A 73-year-old man with sudden onset of a painless, curtain-like loss of vision in the right eye, preceded by flashes of light and floaters.
criterion_text: Patients with uncomplicated rhegmatogenous retinal detcahment.

patient_text: A 60-year-old woman presents with progressive dyspnea, dry cough, and clubbing of the fingers. Chest CT reveals bilateral basal reticular opacities with honeycombing.
criterion_text: History of interstitial lung disease

patient_text: A 3-month-old infant with projectile non-bilious vomiting after feeding, visible peristalsis, and a palpable olive-shaped mass in the epigastrium. Labs show hypochloremic, hypokalemic metabolic alkalosis.
criterion_text: Patient affiliated to the French Social Security system or benefiting from an equivalent plan

patient_text: A 7-year-old boy with a 3-week history of periorbital edema and frothy urine. Urinalysis shows heavy proteinuria without hematuria, and serum albumin is low.
criterion_text: Adult (>18 years) males and females

patient_text: A 19-year-old male presents with fever, sore throat, fatigue, and posterior cervical lymphadenopathy. Physical exam reveals splenomegaly, and a monospot test is positive.
criterion_text: Patients with known or suspected CAEBV must be 1 year of age or older

patient_text: A 34-year-old woman presents with recurrent episodes of severe unilateral throbbing headache preceded by visual scotomata, accompanied by photophobia, nausea, and phonophobia.
criterion_text: Presence of psychiatric, neurological, endocrinological and/or developmental conditions and taking medications for these conditions

patient_text: A 34-year-old woman presents with recurrent episodes of severe unilateral throbbing headache preceded by visual scotomata, accompanied by photophobia, nausea, and phonophobia.
criterion_text: Intracranial vascular malformations or dural AVF

patient_text: A 7-year-old boy with a 3-week history of periorbital edema and frothy urine. Urinalysis shows heavy proteinuria without hematuria, and serum albumin is low.
criterion_text: Secondary

patient_text: A 60-year-old woman presents with progressive dyspnea, dry cough, and clubbing of the fingers. Chest CT reveals bilateral basal reticular opacities with honeycombing.
criterion_text: Subjects over the age of 90 years old

patient_text: A 60-year-old woman presents with progressive dyspnea, dry cough, and clubbing of the fingers. Chest CT reveals bilateral basal reticular opacities with honeycombing.
criterion_text: Diffusing capacity of the lung for carbon monoxide percent predicted (DLCO% predicted), corrected for hemoglobin (Hb), ≥30% and ≤90% during the screening period.

patient_text: A 73-year-old man with sudden onset of a painless, curtain-like loss of vision in the right eye, preceded by flashes of light and floaters.
criterion_text: Able to understand and sign an informed consent form (ICF) that has been approved by an ethics committee.

patient_text: A 68-year-old man with a long smoking history presents with painless gross hematuria. CT urography reveals a mass in the bladder wall.
criterion_text: Uncontrolled intercurrent illness, including but not limited to, ongoing or active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, uncontrolled hypertension, unstable angina pectoris, cardiac arrhythmia, interstitial lung disease, serious chronic gastrointestinal conditions associated with diarrhea, as determined per treating physician

patient_text: A 45-year-old man with poorly controlled type 2 diabetes presents with fever, facial pain, black necrotic eschar on the nasal mucosa, and periorbital swelling.
criterion_text: Age: Children and adults from 3 to 64 years old (18 to 64 years old for controls)

patient_text: A 45-year-old man with poorly controlled type 2 diabetes presents with fever, facial pain, black necrotic eschar on the nasal mucosa, and periorbital swelling.
criterion_text: The subject and his/her guardian are willing to comply with the procedures and operations specified in the study protocol

patient_text: A 73-year-old man with sudden onset of a painless, curtain-like loss of vision in the right eye, preceded by flashes of light and floaters.
criterion_text: Past history of phacovitrectomy in the planned operative eye.

patient_text: A 7-year-old boy with a 3-week history of periorbital edema and frothy urine. Urinalysis shows heavy proteinuria without hematuria, and serum albumin is low.
criterion_text: Inability or unwillingness to comply with repeated assessments

patient_text: A 45-year-old man with poorly controlled type 2 diabetes presents with fever, facial pain, black necrotic eschar on the nasal mucosa, and periorbital swelling.
criterion_text: The subject is known to be allergic to any azole antifungal therapy or other ingredients contained in the study drug

patient_text: A 54-year-old man with a history of chronic alcohol use presents with severe epigastric pain radiating to the back, nausea, and vomiting. Labs reveal markedly elevated serum lipase and amylase.
criterion_text: Biliary pancreatitis diagnosed on imaging (ultrasound, magnetic resonance imaging and/or tomography)

patient_text: A 45-year-old man with poorly controlled type 2 diabetes presents with fever, facial pain, black necrotic eschar on the nasal mucosa, and periorbital swelling.
criterion_text: The researcher believes that the condition of the child may interfere with study participation or other inappropriate conditions

patient_text: A 34-year-old woman presents with recurrent episodes of severe unilateral throbbing headache preceded by visual scotomata, accompanied by photophobia, nausea, and phonophobia.
criterion_text: Patients willing and able to provide simple consent for registry participation.

patient_text: A 54-year-old man with a history of chronic alcohol use presents with severe epigastric pain radiating to the back, nausea, and vomiting. Labs reveal markedly elevated serum lipase and amylase.
criterion_text: Recovery of pancreatitis by tolerance of oral intake (defined as 24 hours of food consumption of any consistency without emetic episodes and pain defined as 4/10 on the visual analogue score of pain)

patient_text: A 3-month-old infant with projectile non-bilious vomiting after feeding, visible peristalsis, and a palpable olive-shaped mass in the epigastrium. Labs show hypochloremic, hypokalemic metabolic alkalosis.
criterion_text: Evidence of active or prior Crohn's disease.

patient_text: A 34-year-old woman presents with recurrent episodes of severe unilateral throbbing headache preceded by visual scotomata, accompanied by photophobia, nausea, and phonophobia.
criterion_text: Able to provide proof of active intrauterine pregnancy and estimated due date, via online document upload, using any of: ultrasound report of current pregnancy, beta hcg lab report, a signed provider's medical documentation confirming intrauterine pregnancy with fetal heart sounds and estimated due date, or a signed provider's note on a prescription sheet or physician letterhead confirming intrauterine pregnancy and estimated due date.

patient_text: A 73-year-old man with sudden onset of a painless, curtain-like loss of vision in the right eye, preceded by flashes of light and floaters.
criterion_text: Pregnant.

patient_text: A 29-year-old woman presents with intermittent palpitations, heat intolerance, and unintentional weight loss. Physical exam reveals a fine tremor, tachycardia, and a diffusely enlarged, non-tender thyroid gland.
criterion_text: Traumatic brain injury with unconsciousness

patient_text: A 3-month-old infant with projectile non-bilious vomiting after feeding, visible peristalsis, and a palpable olive-shaped mass in the epigastrium. Labs show hypochloremic, hypokalemic metabolic alkalosis.
criterion_text: Known immunocompromised status including, but not limited to, individuals who have undergone organ transplantation, chemotherapy or radiotherapy within the past 12 months, who have clinically significant leukopenia, who are positive for the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) or whose immune status makes the subject a poor candidate for clinical trial participation in the opinion of the Investigator.

patient_text: A 29-year-old woman presents with intermittent palpitations, heat intolerance, and unintentional weight loss. Physical exam reveals a fine tremor, tachycardia, and a diffusely enlarged, non-tender thyroid gland.
criterion_text: Patients with heart failure

patient_text: A 19-year-old male presents with fever, sore throat, fatigue, and posterior cervical lymphadenopathy. Physical exam reveals splenomegaly, and a monospot test is positive.
criterion_text: Presence of organ failure

patient_text: A 7-year-old boy with a 3-week history of periorbital edema and frothy urine. Urinalysis shows heavy proteinuria without hematuria, and serum albumin is low.
criterion_text: Meets the 2021 KDIGO definition of steroid-resistant nephrotic syndrome (SRNS), and either: has received an adequate dose of calcineurin inhibitors (CNIs) for more than 6 months without achieving at least partial remission; or has contraindications to CNI use (e.g., eGFR < 60 mL/min/1.73 m², prominent tubular injury on biopsy, elevated urinary markers > 3x ULN, abnormal glucose tolerance, severe uncontrolled hypertension, significant drug interactions, or known CNI allergy); or has shown inadequate response or relapse after treatment with at least two immunosuppressive agents (including a CNI and at least one other conventional or biologic agent), defined as failure to achieve complete remission after 12 months of therapy or relapse following initial response
